content: Next.js App Router search pitfalls without search index: (1) Client-side filtering of large lists blocks the main thread during typing; filter must be debounced and virtualized for lists over ~200 items. (2) useSearchParams() requires Suspense boundary in App Router — missing Suspense causes build error and full-page loading state. (3) URL search params not connected to client state causes back-button to silently break; use nuqs or router.push with shallow:false to keep them in sync. (4) nuqs shallow:true (default) skips server component re-render — filtering that depends on server data must use shallow:false. (5) cmdk/command palette components are client-only; importing without dynamic import or 'use client' causes SSR hydration mismatch. (6) Searching across machines (multi-machine dashboard context) requires a debounced API call or pre-loaded index, not inline filter — inline filter over WebSocket-pushed state can produce stale results mid-update.
